Implements a simple menu layer for UI with functions for initialization, drawing, and interaction.

# Purpose
The code defines a set of functions and structures for managing a simple menu layer in a graphical user interface, likely for an embedded system or application with limited display capabilities. It provides functionality to create, initialize, and manage a `SimpleMenuLayer`, which is a specialized menu component that can display sections and items with optional headers, titles, subtitles, and icons. The code includes functions to handle drawing operations, such as [`draw_row`](<#draw_row>) and [`draw_header`](<#draw_header>), and user interactions, such as [`select_click`](<#select_click>), which triggers a callback when a menu item is selected.

The [`simple_menu_layer_create`](<#simple_menu_layer_create>) function allocates memory for a `SimpleMenuLayer` and initializes it using [`simple_menu_layer_init`](<#simple_menu_layer_init>). The initialization process sets up the menu layer with the provided sections and callback context, and configures the menu layer's callbacks for handling various menu operations. The code also includes functions for deinitializing and destroying a `SimpleMenuLayer`, as well as utility functions to get and set the selected index of the menu. The [`simple_menu_layer_get_layer`](<#simple_menu_layer_get_layer>) and [`simple_menu_layer_get_menu_layer`](<#simple_menu_layer_get_menu_layer>) functions provide access to the underlying `Layer` and `MenuLayer` structures, respectively, allowing for further customization or integration with other components.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `simple_menu_layer.h`
- `applib/legacy2/ui/menu_layer_legacy2.h`
- `applib/applib_malloc.auto.h`
- `process_management/process_manager.h`


# Functions

---
### get\_header\_height<!-- {{#callable:get_header_height}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/simple_menu_layer.c#L23>)

Determines the height of a menu section header based on the presence of a title.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``menu_layer``: A pointer to a `MenuLayer` structure, which is not used in this function.
    - ``section_index``: An unsigned integer representing the index of the section in the menu.
    - ``callback_context``: A pointer to a `SimpleMenuLayer` structure, which contains the sections of the menu.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Ignore the `menu_layer` parameter as it is not used.
    - Check if the `title` field of the section at `section_index` in the `SimpleMenuLayer` pointed to by `callback_context` is not NULL.
    - If the `title` is present, return `MENU_CELL_BASIC_HEADER_HEIGHT`.
    - If the `title` is not present, return 0.
- **Output**: Returns an `int16_t` representing the height of the header: `MENU_CELL_BASIC_HEADER_HEIGHT` if the section has a title, otherwise 0.


---
### get\_num\_sections<!-- {{#callable:get_num_sections}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/simple_menu_layer.c#L32>)

Returns the number of sections in a `SimpleMenuLayer`.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``menu_layer``: A pointer to a `MenuLayer` object, which is not used in this function.
    - ``callback_context``: A pointer to a `SimpleMenuLayer` object, which contains the `num_sections` field.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Ignore the `menu_layer` parameter by casting it to `void`.
    - Cast `callback_context` to a `SimpleMenuLayer` pointer.
    - Access the `num_sections` field from the `SimpleMenuLayer` object.
    - Return the value of `num_sections`.
- **Output**: The function returns a `uint16_t` value representing the number of sections in the `SimpleMenuLayer`.

### get\_num\_rows<!-- {{#callable:get_num_rows}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/simple_menu_layer.c#L37>)

Returns the number of items in a specified section of a `SimpleMenuLayer`.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``menu_layer``: A pointer to a `MenuLayer` object, which is not used in this function.
    - ``section_index``: An unsigned 16-bit integer representing the index of the section in the menu.
    - ``callback_context``: A pointer to a `SimpleMenuLayer` object, which contains the sections and their items.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Ignore the `menu_layer` parameter as it is not used in the function.
    - Cast the `callback_context` to a `SimpleMenuLayer` pointer.
    - Access the `sections` array of the `SimpleMenuLayer` using the `section_index`.
    - Return the `num_items` of the specified section.
- **Output**: An unsigned 16-bit integer representing the number of items in the specified section of the `SimpleMenuLayer`.

### draw\_row<!-- {{#callable:draw_row}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/simple_menu_layer.c#L42>)

Renders a menu row with its title, subtitle, and icon on the display.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``ctx``: A pointer to the `GContext` used for drawing operations.
    - ``cell_layer``: A pointer to the `Layer` representing the cell to draw.
    - ``cell_index``: A pointer to the `MenuIndex` indicating the section and row of the menu item to draw.
    - ``callback_context``: A pointer to the `SimpleMenuLayer` context containing menu data.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Cast `callback_context` to `SimpleMenuLayer` to access menu data.
    - Retrieve the `SimpleMenuItem` from the `SimpleMenuLayer` using `cell_index` to locate the correct section and row.
    - Call `menu_cell_basic_draw` with the context, cell layer, and item details (title, subtitle, icon) to render the menu row.
- **Output**: No return value; the function performs drawing operations on the provided context.


---
### draw\_header<!-- {{#callable:draw_header}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/simple_menu_layer.c#L50>)

Draws the header for a menu section using the title from the `SimpleMenuLayer`.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``ctx``: A pointer to the `GContext` used for drawing.
    - ``cell_layer``: A pointer to the `Layer` where the header will be drawn.
    - ``section_index``: An unsigned 16-bit integer representing the index of the section whose header is to be drawn.
    - ``callback_context``: A pointer to the context, expected to be a `SimpleMenuLayer`, which contains the menu sections and their titles.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Retrieve the title of the section at `section_index` from the `SimpleMenuLayer` pointed to by `callback_context`.
    - Check if the `title` is `NULL`; if it is, return immediately without drawing.
    - If the `title` is not `NULL`, call `menu_cell_basic_header_draw` to draw the header with the given `ctx`, `cell_layer`, and `title`.
- **Output**: No output is returned; the function performs drawing operations on the provided `GContext` and `Layer`.

### select\_click<!-- {{#callable:select_click}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/simple_menu_layer.c#L58>)

Executes a callback function associated with a menu item when it is selected.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``menu_layer``: A pointer to the `MenuLayer` structure, which is not used in this function.
    - ``cell_index``: A pointer to the `MenuIndex` structure that specifies the section and row of the selected menu item.
    - ``callback_context``: A pointer to the `SimpleMenuLayer` structure, which contains the menu sections and items.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Cast `callback_context` to a `SimpleMenuLayer` pointer and store it in `simple_menu`.
    - Retrieve the callback function `cb` from the `simple_menu` using the section and row indices from `cell_index`.
    - Check if `cb` is not `NULL`.
    - If `cb` is not `NULL`, call `cb` with the row index and the `callback_context` from `simple_menu`.
- **Output**: No return value; the function executes a callback if it is defined.

### simple\_menu\_layer\_init<!-- {{#callable:simple_menu_layer_init}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/simple_menu_layer.c#L69>)

Initializes a `SimpleMenuLayer` with specified parameters and sets up menu callbacks.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``simple_menu``: A pointer to the `SimpleMenuLayer` to initialize.
    - ``frame``: A pointer to a `GRect` defining the frame of the menu layer.
    - ``window``: A pointer to the `Window` where the menu layer will be displayed.
    - ``sections``: A pointer to an array of `SimpleMenuSection` structures defining the menu sections.
    - ``num_sections``: An integer representing the number of sections in the menu.
    - ``callback_context``: A pointer to user-defined data to pass to callbacks.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Checks if the process manager is compiled with the legacy SDK using `process_manager_compiled_with_legacy2_sdk()`.
    - Initializes the menu layer using `menu_layer_legacy2_init()` if legacy SDK is used, otherwise uses `menu_layer_init()`.
    - Assigns the `sections`, `num_sections`, and `callback_context` to the `simple_menu`.
    - Sets the menu layer callbacks using `menu_layer_set_callbacks()` with functions for getting number of sections, header height, number of rows, drawing rows, handling select clicks, and drawing headers.
    - Configures the click configuration of the menu layer to the specified `window` using `menu_layer_set_click_config_onto_window()`.
- **Output**: None (void function).
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`menu_layer_legacy2_init`](<../legacy2/ui/menu_layer_legacy2.c.md#menu_layer_legacy2_init>)

### simple\_menu\_layer\_create<!-- {{#callable:simple_menu_layer_create}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/simple_menu_layer.c#L95>)

Creates and initializes a `SimpleMenuLayer` object with specified parameters.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``frame``: A `GRect` structure that defines the size and position of the menu layer.
    - ``window``: A pointer to a `Window` object where the menu layer will be displayed.
    - ``sections``: A pointer to an array of `SimpleMenuSection` structures that define the sections of the menu.
    - ``num_sections``: An `int32_t` value that specifies the number of sections in the menu.
    - ``callback_context``: A pointer to a context that is passed to callback functions.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Allocate memory for a `SimpleMenuLayer` object using `applib_type_malloc`.
    - Check if the memory allocation was successful.
    - If successful, call [`simple_menu_layer_init`](<#simple_menu_layer_init>) to initialize the `SimpleMenuLayer` with the provided parameters.
    - Return the pointer to the initialized `SimpleMenuLayer` object.
- **Output**: A pointer to the newly created and initialized `SimpleMenuLayer` object, or `NULL` if memory allocation fails.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`simple_menu_layer_init`](<#simple_menu_layer_init>)

[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/simple_menu_layer.c#L105>)

- **Inputs**:
    - `menu_layer`: A pointer to the `SimpleMenuLayer` that needs deinitialization.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls the `menu_layer_deinit` function with the `menu` member of the `SimpleMenuLayer` structure.
- **Output**: No return value.

### simple\_menu\_layer\_destroy<!-- {{#callable:simple_menu_layer_destroy}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/simple_menu_layer.c#L109>)

Releases resources allocated for a `SimpleMenuLayer` object.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``menu_layer``: A pointer to a `SimpleMenuLayer` object to be destroyed.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Check if `menu_layer` is `NULL`; if true, return immediately.
    - Call [`simple_menu_layer_deinit`](<#simple_menu_layer_deinit>) to deinitialize the `menu_layer`.
    - Call `applib_free` to free the memory allocated for `menu_layer`.
- **Output**: No return value.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`simple_menu_layer_deinit`](<#simple_menu_layer_deinit>)

### simple\_menu\_layer\_get\_layer<!-- {{#callable:simple_menu_layer_get_layer}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/simple_menu_layer.c#L117>)

Retrieves the `Layer` associated with a `SimpleMenuLayer`.
- **Inputs**:
    - `simple_menu`: A pointer to a `SimpleMenuLayer` from which to retrieve the `Layer`.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Call [`menu_layer_get_layer`](<menu_layer.c.md#menu_layer_get_layer>) with the address of the `menu` member of `simple_menu`.
    - Return the result of [`menu_layer_get_layer`](<menu_layer.c.md#menu_layer_get_layer>).
- **Output**: A pointer to a `Layer` object associated with the given `SimpleMenuLayer`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`menu_layer_get_layer`](<menu_layer.c.md#menu_layer_get_layer>)

### simple\_menu\_layer\_get\_selected\_index<!-- {{#callable:simple_menu_layer_get_selected_index}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/simple_menu_layer.c#L121>)

Retrieves the row index of the currently selected item in a `SimpleMenuLayer`.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``simple_menu``: A pointer to a `SimpleMenuLayer` from which to get the selected index.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls [`menu_layer_get_selected_index`](<menu_layer.c.md#menu_layer_get_selected_index>) with the `menu` member of `simple_menu`.
    - Returns the `row` attribute of the `MenuIndex` structure obtained from [`menu_layer_get_selected_index`](<menu_layer.c.md#menu_layer_get_selected_index>).
- **Output**: The row index of the currently selected item in the `SimpleMenuLayer`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`menu_layer_get_selected_index`](<menu_layer.c.md#menu_layer_get_selected_index>)

### simple\_menu\_layer\_set\_selected\_index<!-- {{#callable:simple_menu_layer_set_selected_index}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/simple_menu_layer.c#L125>)

Sets the selected index of a `SimpleMenuLayer` to a specified row index, with an option to animate the change.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``simple_menu``: A pointer to a `SimpleMenuLayer` structure that represents the menu layer to modify.
    - ``index``: An `int32_t` value representing the new row index to select within the current section of the menu.
    - ``animated``: A `bool` indicating whether the change of selection should be animated (`true`) or not (`false`).
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Creates a [`MenuIndex`](<menu_layer.h.md#MenuIndex>) structure using the current section index from `simple_menu` and the provided `index`.
    - Calls `menu_layer_set_selected_index` with the `simple_menu`'s menu, the created [`MenuIndex`](<menu_layer.h.md#MenuIndex>), `MenuRowAlignCenter` for alignment, and the `animated` flag to set the new selected index.
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the state of the `SimpleMenuLayer` directly.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`MenuIndex`](<menu_layer.h.md#MenuIndex>)

### simple\_menu\_layer\_get\_menu\_layer<!-- {{#callable:simple_menu_layer_get_menu_layer}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/simple_menu_layer.c#L130>)

Returns a pointer to the `menu` member of a `SimpleMenuLayer` structure.
- **Inputs**:
    - `simple_menu`: A pointer to a `SimpleMenuLayer` structure from which to retrieve the `menu` member.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Accesses the `menu` member of the `SimpleMenuLayer` structure pointed to by `simple_menu`.
    - Returns the address of the `menu` member.
- **Output**: A pointer to the `MenuLayer` member of the given `SimpleMenuLayer`.
